title: NOC 72020 – Contractors and supervisors, mechanic trades | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Contractors and Supervisors in Mechanic Trades (NOC 72020) are the essential technical leaders maintaining Canada’s industrial and transportation infrastructure. From aviation and rail to heavy construction and high-tech manufacturing, these professionals manage the specialized mechanics who keep Canada moving. Classified as TEER 2, this role demands a high level of technical mastery—typically as a Journeyperson—paired with the leadership skills required to manage complex maintenance schedules and high-stakes safety protocols in 2026’s increasingly automated industrial environments.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group720 – Technical trades contractors and supervisors

duties: Operational Coordination: Supervising, coordinating, and scheduling the activities of workers who repair and maintain aircraft, locomotives, industrial machinery, or motor vehicles.
Technical Troubleshooting: Establishing methods to meet work schedules and resolving complex technical problems to improve productivity and quality.
Resource Management: Requisitioning materials and supplies and ensuring all specialized tools and diagnostic equipment are calibrated to 2026 standards.
Training & Safety: Training or arranging for the training of workers and ensuring that all standards for safe working conditions (including Occupational Health and Safety) are strictly observed.
Reporting: Preparing work progress and production reports and recommending personnel actions such as hiring or promotions.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Experience: Several years of experience as a qualified Journeyperson in a relevant trade (e.g., Heavy Duty Equipment Technician, AME, or Automotive Service Tech) are mandatory.
Certification: Journeyperson trade certification in a relevant mechanic trade is required.
Leadership Skills: Proficiency in Computer-Aided Maintenance Systems (CAMS) and digital workflow management software.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Strong, fueled by the modernization of Canada’s transport fleets and a critical shortage of experienced industrial leaders:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. On March 25, 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72020 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. Minimum scores were highly competitive, including 53 in Southwestern Ontario and 54 in Northern Ontario, reflecting the province's urgent need for mechanics supervisors in the automotive and industrial hubs.

Alberta: Highest Demand. Alberta remains the top-paying province for this NOC, with a median wage of $46.00/hr ($96,000+ annually) and top-tier earners reaching $70.00/hr in the energy and heavy equipment sectors.

British Columbia: Good prospects. Demand is steady in Vancouver and the Okanagan for aircraft maintenance and heavy-duty equipment supervisors. BC projects over 2,500 openings through 2035 due to a wave of retirements.

National Trend: Over 90% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time. With roughly 37% of the workforce currently over age 50, the next decade represents a significant opportunity for international supervisors to fill the "leadership gap."
Why Work in Canada as a Mechanics Supervisor?
Lead Industrial AI Integration: Manage the transition to Predictive Maintenance systems, where AI sensors detect machinery failures before they happen.
Diverse Sector Opportunities: Your skills are vital across aviation, railways, mining, automotive repair, and renewable energy plants (wind/hydro turbines).
High-Tier Compensation: As a TEER 2 supervisor, you earn significantly more than general mechanics, with median wages at $40.00/hr and high-end industrial roles exceeding $60.00/hr.
Priority for PR: In early 2026, this NOC has been a consistent highlight in Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) draws and is a primary target for the 2026 TR to PR transition streams.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): On March 25, 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72020 under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a high-success route for those with an Ontario job offer.

2026 TR to PR Pathway: A newly launched federal initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents with skilled trade experience. Mechanics supervisors are classified as "High-Priority Industrial Workers."
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Trades): As a TEER 2 trade, it is eligible for category-based draws for skilled trades, which typically have much lower CRS requirements than general draws.
Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot: A streamlined pathway for technical machine operators and supervisors to obtain PR with a provincial job offer.
